text:
BREAST, LEFT, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Invasive carcinoma of no special type (NST), grade 2. ER positive (95%), PR positive (80%), HER2 negative. Ki67 approximately 15%.

BREAST, RIGHT, EXCISIONAL BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS), high nuclear grade with comedo necrosis. No invasive carcinoma identified. Margins negative.

BREAST, LEFT, STEREOTACTIC CORE BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Atypical ductal hyperplasia (ADH). No in-situ or invasive carcinoma identified. Recommend excision for further evaluation.

BREAST, RIGHT, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Fibroadenoma with focal adenosis. No atypia, no in-situ or invasive carcinoma.

BREAST, LEFT, LUMPECTOMY

Final Diagnosis: Invasive lobular carcinoma, grade 1, classic type. Tumor size 1.4 cm. All surgical margins negative (minimum clearance 4 mm). Three sentinel lymph nodes negative.

BREAST, RIGHT, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Metastatic melanoma to breast parenchyma. Immunostains positive for S100, SOX10, and Melan-A. Negative for cytokeratins and GATA3. Clinical correlation recommended for primary site.

BREAST, LEFT, EXCISIONAL BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Spindle cell neoplasm, cannot exclude phyllodes tumor. Features overlap between cellular fibroadenoma and low-grade phyllodes tumor. Recommend re-excision with wider margins and specialist review.

BREAST, BILATERAL, REDUCTION MAMMOPLASTY

Final Diagnosis: Bilateral breast tissue with fibrocystic changes, columnar cell change, and mild usual ductal hyperplasia. No atypia or malignancy identified.

AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, RIGHT, SENTINEL NODE BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: One sentinel lymph node negative for malignancy (0/1). No evidence of metastatic carcinoma on H&E or cytokeratin immunostain.

BREAST, LEFT, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS), classic type. No invasive carcinoma identified. Clinical and radiologic correlation required for management.

BREAST, RIGHT, MASTECTOMY

Final Diagnosis: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), non-germinal center subtype, involving breast parenchyma. CD20 positive, BCL2 positive, MYC positive in 60% of cells. Ki67 80%. No invasive carcinoma.

BREAST, RIGHT, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED BIOPSY

Final Diagnosis: Core biopsy of breast — see comment.

Comment: Sections show fibrocollagenous tissue with no epithelial elements identified. The findings are discordant with the imaging impression of a 1.5 cm hypoechoic mass. Repeat biopsy strongly recommended.
